evidence.snippet: Butler Parachute Systems offers a variety of parachute systems for Unmanned Aerial Vehicles. We have the experience, the personnel, and the facilities to design and manufacture a wide variety of vehicle recovery systems.
Butler Parachutes manufactures high-quality parachute systems for emergency egress, military missions, cargo delivery, and UAV systems.

items.notes: Primary product lines include UAV parachute recovery systems, personnel emergency parachutes, and cargo delivery parachutes.

evidence.snippet: Butler Parachute Systems has the capability to test parachutes and recovery systems from sub-munitions size up to approximately 4,000 lb. at speeds up to 850 knots.

items.notes: Test services offered to external customers including drop-tower testing, aircraft drop tests, and data collection; secondary to manufacturing.

evidence.snippet: Our newly renovated, 21,000 sq. ft. manufacturing and testing facility is located just west of Roanoke, Virginia.
Butler Parachute Systems was established in 1979 by founder Manley C. Butler Jr. It was moved from California City, California, to Roanoke, Virginia in early 1995.

headquarters.notes: Street address 1820 Loudon Avenue NW, Roanoke, VA 24017 appears in the footer of every fetched page.

evidence.snippet: Butler Parachute Systems also specializes in complete recovery systems for unmanned applications, such as Unmanned Air Systems (UAS/UAV), target drones, rockets, and munitions.
Recovery System for the Shadow 200 TUAV: BPS designed and built a recovery system for the Shadow 200 TUAV widely used by the US Army.

drone_supply_chain_role.notes: Butler supplies complete, installable UAV recovery subsystems (canopy + container + deployment mechanism). Primary revenue is emergency parachutes for manned aircraft; UAV recovery is a well-established secondary product line.

evidence.snippet: Our UAV parachute recovery systems come with all the necessary components and equipment to successfully recover unmanned air vehicles (UAVs), target drones, and other air vehicles.

items.name: HX-Series
items.product_type: parachute canopy line (used in UAV and personnel recovery systems)

evidence.snippet: The main canopy (HX-400 – 22' Do) is housed in a BPS designed/manufactured container with a vacuum formed shell, stainless steel stiffener and nylon flaps.

items.notes: HX-400, HX-500, HX-500/24, HX-500/600 are named variants within the HX-Series canopy family.

items.name: Lopo Series

evidence.snippet: Furthermore, in FY 2001-2002, the FS purchased over 700 chest reserve parachutes using the BPS LoPo 550 canopy.

items.notes: LoPo 550 is a named SKU within the Lopo Series product line.

evidence.snippet: The BPS Commercial High Altitude Bailout System was selected by the USAF as the BA-30 Low Profile Parachute System (LPP). The BA-30 LPP replaces all the legacy BA-22 emergency parachutes in use by all US Air Force units.
Butler Parachutes Tethered Tandem Bundle System, the TT-600, has been adopted by the USAF, USN, Sweden, Norway and others. The SMARTChute system and it's components are also widely used.

products.notes: Product pages for HX-Series, Lopo Series, pilot parachutes, and cargo canopies were not fetched; additional named SKUs likely exist.

evidence.snippet: The BPS network is robust and secured under the CMMC standards for protection of CUI information, including ITAR restrictions.

ndaa.notes: Site mentions CMMC and ITAR compliance but makes no NDAA Section 848/889 claim. ITAR-restricted products and CMMC-certified network are adjacent compliance signals only.

blue_uas.notes: No mention of Blue UAS, Blue sUAS, or DIU cleared list found in any fetched page.

evidence.snippet: As of 2010, we have delivered over 1800 recovery systems to the customer.
Butler Parachute Systems, Inc. maintains a robust quality control system. The system was implemented in 2022 in accordance with the ISO 9001:2015 standard. The QMS was re-certified in April 2025.

readiness.notes: Active serial production: FAA-approved under TSO C23, ISO 9001:2015 certified (re-certified 2025), 65+ sewing machines, two CNC cutting tables, US military delivery contracts. Also offers custom engineering/design services as a secondary mode.

unresolved_questions: Blue UAS / DIU cleared list status not mentioned anywhere on site
NDAA Section 848/889 compliance not explicitly stated (only CMMC/ITAR adjacent signals found)
HX-Series and Lopo Series canopy product pages not fetched; full SKU list unknown

fetch_requests.reason: Would provide named HX-Series canopy SKUs and confirm whether any are specifically marketed for UAV recovery, sharpening products and products_categories answers.
Would provide named Lopo Series canopy SKUs and product details, completing the product listing for this canopy family.
Military overview page likely lists all UAV and military recovery system product lines, helping confirm drone_supply_chain_role and complete the products list.
Engineering services page would clarify whether Butler offers UAV-specific design/engineering contracts and confirm readiness classification.

tagline: Manufactures parachute recovery systems for UAVs, pilots, and cargo delivery, including USAF-selected BA-30 low-profile bailout systems and TT-600 tethered bundles adopted across DoD and allied nations. ISO-certified, production-ready. NDAA compliance and Blue UAS certification status unknown; public evidence of military adoption is strong but limited to specific platforms.
